behavior_summary: U8P_LEGRIGHT defines two process handlers for an NPC-like entity: U8P_LEGRIGHT::use (main) and U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit (helper).

U8P_LEGRIGHT::use is invoked when the entity is 'used' and unconditionally spawns a BODYPART helper process (BODYPART::func0270), capturing the spawn handle into a local (temp). This likely starts a visual or behavior sub-process tied to the entity.

U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit runs when the entity is U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit: it calls FREE::resetRef(0x0202, this) to clear an internal reference, then queries Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x020A, this). If no processes of that type exist, it spawns a BODYPART helper (BODYPART::func0431). This prevents duplicate U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit-reaction helpers from being created.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LEGRIGHT::use: unconditional execution path — spawn BODYPART::func0270 and store handle in local 'temp'.
U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit: linear sequence — FREE::resetRef(0x0202, this) -> check Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x020A, this) -> if zero then spawn BODYPART::func0431; otherwise skip spawn.
open_questions: Exact runtime effects and visuals produced by BODYPART::func0270 and BODYPART::func0431 are not present in the excerpt — what animations/effects do these helper processes perform?
What semantic role does reference slot 0x0202 represent (what state is being reset by FREE::resetRef)?
Why Kernel::getNumProcesses checks for process type 0x020A specifically (what class of helper/process is 0x020A)?
Are the U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit parameters (uword, word) used elsewhere in a broader script context (they are present in the signature but not used in the shown nodes)?

main_function.name: U8P_LEGRIGHT::use
main_function.purpose: Start a BODYPART helper process when the entity is activated (likely to trigger a visual/behavior effect tied to 'U8P_LEGRIGHT::use').
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the NPC/entity is 'used' or activated (process type 0x0207).
No in-function conditional gating — spawn happens unconditionally on entry.

function_map.name: U8P_LEGRIGHT::use
function_map.role: Activation handler — spawn a BODYPART helper process to produce a U8P_LEGRIGHT::use-linked behavior/effect.

function_map.name: U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit
function_map.role: Hit reaction handler — reset an internal reference and conditionally spawn a BODYPART U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit helper to avoid duplicates.

external_calls.purpose: Launch a BODYPART helper/process when the entity is used; return value stored in local 'temp' (likely a handle).
Clear/reset an internal reference slot (id 0x0202) for this entity before handling U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit logic.
Query how many processes of type 0x020A are running for this entity; used as a guard to avoid spawning duplicate U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit helper processes.
Conditionally spawn a BODYPART helper/process to perform a U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit reaction/effect when no existing 0x020A processes are present.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_LEGRIGHT defines two process handlers for an NPC-like entity: U8P_LEGRIGHT::use (main) and U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit (helper).
